items.q: What does a concave lens do to parallel rays of light?
items.correct: It diverges them — they spread out as if from a virtual focal point behind the lens.
items.misconception: It converges them to a focal point in front of the lens.
items.random_wrong: It reflects them back the way they came.
items.why: Students mix up convex (converging) and concave (diverging) lenses because the names are easy to confuse and the diagrams look similar.

items.q: Why does a metal surface feel colder than a wooden surface in the same room?
items.correct: Metal conducts heat away from your hand more quickly than wood, so it removes thermal energy faster — even though both are at room temperature.
items.misconception: The metal is actually colder than the wood.
items.random_wrong: The metal absorbs the heat from the air, leaving it colder than wood.
items.why: Students equate 'feeling cold' with 'being cold', missing that the sensation is about heat flow rate rather than absolute temperature.

items.q: What absorber is needed to stop beta radiation?
items.correct: A few millimetres of aluminium (or similar thin metal sheet).
items.misconception: A sheet of paper — beta is only slightly more penetrating than alpha.
items.random_wrong: Thick lead — only lead can stop any radiation.
items.why: Students remember 'paper, aluminium, lead' as a trio but mix up which goes with which radiation type.

items.q: Redshift of distant galaxies is evidence that:
items.why: Correct answer: the universe is expanding. “the universe is contracting” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The Sun is currently in which stage?
items.why: Correct answer: Main sequence. “Protostar” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If the universe is 13.8 billion years old, how many seconds is that? (1 year ≈ 3.15×10⁷ s; answer in 10¹⁷ s)
items.why: The real figure is 4.35 x10^17 s. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
